item.title: Rohingya crisis needs political solution: FM
item.description: Foreign minister Khalilur Rahman said on Thursday that Rohingya crisis was a political failure and it needed a political solution......

item.title: ACC seeks docs from PDB, BPC over graft allegations against Summit Group
item.description: The Anti-Corruption Commission has sought documents from the Bangladesh Power Development Board and Bangladesh Petroleum Corporation as part of an inquiry into allegations of corruption and money laundering involving Summit Group’s power plants and fuel imports......GeographicReference

item.title: Floods in northeast India kill 41
item.description: Floods triggered by torrential monsoon rains have killed at least 41 people in India’s northeast Assam state, officials said Thursday, as authorities pursued rescue and relief operations......

item.title: Saudi nuclear deal contingent on recognising Israel: Trump
item.description: US president Donald Trump said on Thursday that a deal with Saudi Arabia to establish a civilian nuclear programme would be contingent on the kingdom’s recognition of Israel, a move long sought by Washington but resisted by Riyadh......

item.title: Policy gap, staff crisis stymie Financial Reporting Council
item.description: The Financial Reporting Council has remained largely ineffective in carrying out its core task of overseeing accounting and auditing standards in public-interest entities despite being in operation since 2015......

item.title: Bangladesh wishes India quick solution to crisis
item.description: State minister for foreign affairs Shama Obaed Islam on Thursday said that Bangladesh hoped that India would take into account the situation faced by its people and act swiftly to resolve the matter......Environmentclimate change

item.title: Flood shelters ready as rivers swell in northeast
item.description: Scores of flood shelters have been opened in northeastern districts as rivers continued to swell on Thursday amid forecasts of intermittent heavy to very heavy rainfall that could trigger fresh flooding......

item.title: India’s CJP to protest nationwide today
item.description: As youth protests spread to several Indian cities, the Cockroach Janta Party has formally called for nationwide protests on Friday, demanding the resignation of the education minister, reports DW.com......

item.title: Authorities struggle to fix fault-hit LNG terminal
item.description: People and businesses across the country continued to suffer on Thursday as authorities struggled to restore operations at a malfunctioning floating liquefied natural gas terminal off Cox’s Bazar, with no clear indication of when normal gas supply would resume......

item.title: Gas crisis threatens widespread industrial closures
item.description: The energy crisis that has plagued the country’s industries for more than a decade is now taking a heavier toll, as persistent gas shortages push factories beyond reduced production into outright closure, stoking fears of a wider collapse across the export-oriented manufacturing sector......News

item.title: Govt should rethink power, fuel privatisation plans
item.description: THE government mulls over privatising power distribution and petroleum product import. The power, energy and mineral resources minister, at a policy conclave in Dhaka on July 22, said that the objective of the move is to reduce the financial burden on the national exchequer by placing retail energy services in the private sector. The minister has said that the government......

item.title: An attack that authorities mustn’t ignore
item.description: THE vandalism of the country’s first monument dedicated to women, who took part in, led and sacrificed for the July uprising, is an attack on the nation’s collective memory, its democratic aspirations and the rightful place of women in its history. The destruction of the sculpted female figures at the July Shaheed Nari Mancha at Uttara comes months after the postponement......

item.title: Every child deserves equal start
item.description: A child’s future is shaped long before they enter a classroom. Just as the morning foretells the day, the quality of early childhood determines a person’s lifelong opportunities, wellbeing and success. A strong start means growing up in a safe, nurturing and stimulating environment where children enjoy their fundamental rights to health, nutrition, protection and......

item.title: Education needs more than 2pc of GDP
item.description: A COUNTRY shows its priorities in its budget. If Bangladesh is serious about building a skilled work force, reducing corruption and becoming a developed economy, education cannot continue to receive less than 2 per cent of the gross domestic product. No country has produced a world-class generation by treating education as a secondary investment......

item.title: Rice of Bengal and politics of approval
item.description: FOR thousands of years, Bengal was not merely a place where rice was grown. It was one of the great rice civilisations of the world......

item.title: Escalation trap: from Vietnam to Iran
item.description: ON FEBRUARY 28, 2026, the United States and Israel launched military operations against Iran. A fragile two-week ceasefire followed on April 8, later extended under the Islamabad Memorandum of Understanding signed by the US and Iran on June 18. Yet the ceasefire has not ended the conflict. The fundamental drivers of the war — Iran’s nuclear ambitions......

item.title: Demolition of college Shaheed Minar in Dhaka stirs fresh controversy
item.description: Demolition of a Shaheed Minar inside a non-government college in the capital’s Puran Paltan area has sparked fresh controversy on social media......News

item.title: Jailed namesake gets Tk 30 lakh in damages
item.description: A man who spent nearly four months in jail after being wrongly arrested in a cheque dishonour case due to a similarity in names has received Tk 30 lakh in compensation......

item.title: Policy sought for battery-run rickshaws, easy bikes
item.description: Politicians, labour leaders and professionals at a roundtable in Dhaka on Thursday called on the Bangladesh Nationalist Party-led alliance government to urgently formulate a coordinated policy for battery-run rickshaw and easy bikes currently running across the country......

item.title: RU blockade cost Tk 35 lakh, affected 20,000 passengers: railway
item.description: Bangladesh Railway claimed that Tuesday’s rail blockade by Rajshahi University students over the alleged assault of a fellow student disrupted services across the western region, affecting about 20,000 passengers and causing revenue losses of about Tk 35 lakh......

item.title: People rose up together in July uprising: Azfar Hussain
item.description: The July Uprising should be viewed as a unique historical moment, distinct from its aftermath, characterised by non-partisan and non-hierarchical creative leadership, critic and academic professor Azfar Hussain said at a public lecture on Thursday......GeographicReference

item.title: 63 eminent citizens demand constitution reform council
item.description: Sixty-three prominent citizens, including politicians and academia, on Thursday demanded the formation of a constitution reform council within the 180-day timeframe stipulated in the National July Charter adopted last year......

item.title: BNP blamed for ignoring July charter
item.description: The BNP-led government at a memorial programme on Thursday was accused of showing little interest in implementing the July charter and of abandoning the reform agenda that emerged from the July uprising......

item.title: Govt reviewing coastal building limits: minister
item.description: Initiatives have been taken to review existing restrictions on construction within 50 to 500 metres of the shoreline, finance and planning minister Amir Khosru Mahmud Chowdhury said on Thursday......

item.title: SEU celebrates World Poetry Day 2026
item.description: Southeast University, in collaboration with Writers Web Bangladesh, celebrated World Poetry Day 2026 on its Tejgaon campus in the capital on Thursday......Environmentclimate change

item.title: Ex-minister Morshed gets 6-month civil detention
item.description: A Dhaka court has issued a six-month civil detention order against former foreign minister and Pacific Bangladesh Telecom Limited director M Morshed Khan in a case filed by One Bank PLC to recover Tk 136.45 crore in defaulted loans......

item.title: Satkhira protesters demand expelled Jamaat MP’s resignation
item.description: A group of residents in Shyamnagar upazila of Satkhira on Thursday staged a human chain, broom procession and protest rally demanding the resignation of Gazi Nazrul Islam, the recently expelled Bangladesh Jamaat-e-Islami lawmaker from the Satkhira-4 constituency......

item.title: IU’s Tk 537cr project remains unfinished after 8 years
item.description: A Tk 537.07 crore infrastructure project at the Islamic University (IU) in Kushtia has remained unfinished eight years after its approval despite three deadline extensions, with the university now seeking a fourth extension as several major structures are still under construction......

item.title: Man gets life term for raping daughter
item.description: A Gazipur tribunal has sentenced a man to life imprisonment and fined him Tk 1 lakh for raping his daughter......ExecutiveBranch

item.title: Pabna dairy fined for making milk with detergent, chemicals
item.description: A mobile court has fined an establishment named ‘Akif Anas Dairy’ Tk 3 lakh for producing toxic milk by mixing detergent and harmful chemicals in Faridpur upazila in Pabna......

item.title: Probe body formed after razor blade found in govt school tiffin
item.description: The government has formed an investigation committee after a razor blade was found inside bread served as tiffin at a government primary school in Gournadi upazila of Barishal, officials said......
